  • Strategic Planning Scenarios in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Universal Model
  • The Future of Autonomous Software Engineering (ASE) in the Enterprise Context
  • Using a Custom Theme Across SAP Platforms and Systems

SAP Concur

  • Concur Budget Voice: Customer Insights & Innovation
  • Investigation into a new ICS feature
  • SAP Business Data Cloud for Concur

Data & Analytics

  • Enhancing the SAP HANA Cloud and SAP Build Code Experience
  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud (XSC,XSA,CAP)
  • The Gen AI Assistant in SAP HANA Cloud
  • Uplift modelling and causality analysis for SAP Analytics  Cloud

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Finance & Risk

  • AI assisted creation of balance validation rules AND comment proposal
  • Financial Reporting according to IFRS18 in S4HANA Cloud
  • Financial Statements in the New Review Booklets Analytical Application

Cloud Delivery & Excellence

  • Potential Workforce Person Data Correction Tool
  • Test Data Variants–Driven Test Process Flow Control and Execution
  • Tool-Agnostic Test Automation from AI-Generated Process Variants for ERP Systems

Digital Supply Chain

  • Empowering Reliability: Data-Driven Maintenance Efficiency Analysis for Strategy Optimization
  • Freight Rate Management for transportation processes with a low to medium complexity
  • Reimagine Warehouse Management with Joule

Private Cloud

  • Best Practice for Inspection Checklists in SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud

Public Cloud

  • Actual Liquidity Analysis
  • AI Agent for Open Item Clearing
  • AI-powered chatbot (Joule) for Intelligent Margin Analysis
  • Assess and improve your Quote-to-Cash process with meaningful Business Health KPIs
  • Enhancing Payment Run Experience
  • Optimizing Partner Solution Support in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Ecosystem
  • Price Recommendation in S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition
  • Research: Payment Advice In Existing Business Process
  • Sales Contract Management - Artificial Intelligent Handling for Quantity/Value Consumption by Joule
  • SAP Business Suite: Agility and Innovation through seamless Integration and Harmonization

User Experience

  • AI-assisted error resolution and data entry for SAP Fiori apps
  • AI-assisted Smart Visualization of MyHome for To-Dos (situations)

Industries

  • Contract Manufacturing Organization Collaboration for CGT Supply Chains using SAP Business Network
  • Real-Time Treatment Scheduling Optimization for Cell and Gene Therapy Orchestration

SAP Integration Experience Feedback

  • Assisted Assessment of End-to-End Implementation based on SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing
  • Contingent & SOW Procurement in SAP Fieldglass: A Hands-on Experience with GenAI Features
  • Hands-on System Experience of Cost Center Integration using SAP MDI service for Source to Pay
  • Hands-on System Experience of integrated Toolchain aiding Business Suite Transformation
  • Hands-on System Experience on the integrated Business Scenario “Travel to Reimburse”

Others

  • Auto discovery of SAP applications and extensions in SAP LeanIX
  • Planned Domain Model Extensibility Portal
  • Shape the future of using AI in Cloud ALM for Operations

Let’s shape the future together!

Innovation happens through collaboration. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, you help SAP create better, more relevant solutions for businesses like yours. This is your chance to influence what comes next.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 2 in 2025!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: New enhancements to RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard
entry.content:
We are excited to announce significant enhancements to the RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard, building upon the application's successful release in January this year. These new updates aim to provide a more comprehensive and insightful experience, empowering you to optimize your SAP S/4HANA system and achieving a clean core. 

**Improved Software Stack Assessment** 

The updated dashboard now offers a more robust software stack assessment, emphasizing the importance of maintaining the latest SAP S/4HANA product version or feature package stack. By ensuring your system is up-to-date, you can leverage SAP-delivered innovations, mitigate security risks, and align with the clean core principles. This enhancement enables you to:  

  • Verify that your system is on the latest SAP S/4HANA product version or feature package stack 
  • Install the latest feature package stack/support package stack to stay current with SAP innovations and security patches 

**Simplified Upgrade Readiness Check** 

Upgrading to SAP S/4HANA has never been more streamlined, thanks to the SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA Upgrades. This innovative tool empowers you to assess your current system's readiness for a seamless upgrade to your desired target SAP S/4HANA release. With a single glance, the SAP Readiness Check card provides a comprehensive overview of the most critical findings from your system's latest assessment, enabling you to gauge your preparedness for the upgrade or estimate the necessary effort. 

**Enhanced Extensibility Information** 

To facilitate a better understanding of your system's extensibility, we have introduced enhanced information on separate software components using the ABAP language version ABAP for Cloud Development. This update helps you: 

  • Determine if a separate software component has been created for building extensions in Tier 1 
  • Gain insight into your system's extensibility architecture and identify potential areas for improvement 

**Unused Customer Objects: A Key Focus Area** 

The updated dashboard now provides a dedicated card to track Unused Customer Objects, which are extensions created outside of the SAP standard. Reducing the number of Unused Customer Objects is a crucial goal of the extensibility dimension. The card displays: 

  • The current share of Unused Customer Objects in your system 
  • The number of Unused Objects and Trackable Objects in your system 

**Execution of Customer Objects: A Measure of Business Process Reliance** 

To assess your efforts for future upgrades and adaptation, the dashboard now offers a card that measures the Execution of Customer Objects. This update helps you: 

  • Understand how heavily your business processes rely on executable customer objects 
  • Compare the frequency of executable customer object execution to the total execution of all executable objects in your system (including SAP, partner, and customer objects) 

**Introducing the Operations View** 

In addition to these enhancements, we are introducing a new view.  

The RISE with SAP Operations View is a dashboard available for RISE with SAP customers designed to deliver daily insights and historical data on system health-related key performance indicators, enabling organizations to efficiently align with the clean core compliance goals from the operations dimension. 

Several metrics are collected from SAP Cloud ALM applications for all your configured, productive  SAP S/4HANA systems and evaluated on a daily basis against specific thresholds. The results of these metric compliance calculations are aggregated and organized in KPIs and scores displayed in the overview and detailed pages.  

Metrics, thresholds and evaluation algorithms are shared ensuring a homogeneous compliance calculation across all RISE with SAP customers. 

***Key Features and Benefits*** 

The Operations View provides daily insights, featuring up to 30 days of historical data available until the day before, for the System Health score and its KPIs: Connectivity, Exceptions, Background Processing and Performance. 

  • Overview of System Health score for all your productive SAP S/4HANA systems (Cloud Private Edition and On Premise) 
  • System-specific view to identify and analyze metrics deviations responsible for each KPI 
  • Jump-in to standard SAP Cloud ALM applications to access the highest level of details 

Overview page of the Operations View Dashboard 

For more information about the Operations View dashboard, see https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/operations-view on the SAP Help Portal and the “Introducing the new RISE with SAP Operations View Dashboard” on the SAP Community Blogs portal. 

These updates demonstrate our ongoing commitment to delivering a comprehensive and actionable RISE with SAP Methodology dashboard. By leveraging these enhancements, you can further optimize your SAP S/4HANA system, achieve a clean core, and drive business success. More details can be found on our Help Portal page: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/applicationhelp/rise-with-sap. 

If you don’t want to miss out any new feature, subscribe to the What’s New viewer:  What's New in SAP Cloud ALM | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Beyond SAP Cloud - Episode 28: Terraform Destroy
entry.content:
In this video, we demonstrate how to easily destroy all resources created with Terraform using a single command. Terraform tracks the state of all created resources, allowing for easy destruction by running `terraform destroy`. The process analyzes the state and removes all resources like sub accounts and directories, returning the environment to its initial state. This feature is useful for creating temporary environments, such as sandboxes for business hours, and ensures that everything can be quickly removed at the end of the day, making it ideal for testing or short-term projects.

Takeaways

  • Terraform maintains the state of what it creates, such as sub accounts and directories.
  • The terraform destroy command analyzes the current state to determine what needs to be destroyed.
  • Running terraform destroy will identify all created resources and prepare them for removal.
  • The script will display what resources are going to be destroyed, such as sub accounts and directories.
  • Once you confirm with 'yes', Terraform will begin destroying the resources it previously created.
  • Terraform can be used to create and destroy temporary environments, like a sandbox for business use during work hours.
  • Destruction may take a few minutes, depending on the complexity of the created resources.
  • After the destroy command completes, everything that was created will be gone, as if nothing ever happened.
  • Terraform's destroy functionality makes it easy to remove all resources without manual intervention.
  • The process demonstrates the convenience of using Terraform to automate the lifecycle of environments, from creation to destruction.
  • Terraform can be used for temporary setups that only need to exist during business hours and can be recreated the next day.

entry.title: SAP BTP Guidance Framework: A Compass for Architects, Developers, and Administrators
entry.content:
Say hello to the SAP BTP Guidance Framework—a practical framework tailored to anyone using the SAP Business Technology Platform. SAP BTP acts as the technological backbone for building, integrating, extending, and innovating with SAP solutions. Whether you're an enterprise architect, software developer, or administrator, SAP BTP offers significant opportunities for productivity and development.

Given the extensive options and tools SAP BTP offers, we created the SAP BTP Guidance Framework to help you fully leverage the platform. This framework serves as your compass, providing comprehensive guidance, simplifying your work, and helping you make smart technology choices.

The Guidance Framework provides clear direction and a structured approach to adopting SAP technologies. With this guidance, you can develop intelligent applications using advanced analytics, equipping your organization with a robust foundation to support agile innovation.

Let’s take a closer look at the main components:

Along your SAP BTP adoption journey, every choice makes a difference. Our Decision Guides help you identify the technology option that best fits your needs by providing navigation for architects, developers and administrators.

Which guides do we offer?

  • Integration architecture guide: supports you in evolving your existing integration architecture.
  • Extension architecture guide: supports the advancement of your existing extension architecture and helps you determine which SAP technology should be used in a given scenario.
  • SAP BTP Developer’s guide: improves the process of implementing a business application on SAP BTP.
  • SAP BTP Administrator’s guide: how- to set up your landscape and lifecycle management for running applications on SAP BTP.
  • SAP BTP Center of Expertise (CoE) guide: centralizing the strategy, governance and operating model to realize the potential of SAP BTP with the CoE, which has just recently moved to the SAP Guidance Framework.

If you are architecting solutions on SAP BTP, we offer curated solution templates. Our Reference Architectures are templates that outline consistent components and configurations for software implementations. They show how applications, data, and AI operate in both cloud and on-premises environments. These templates illustrate how different services interact and integrate with SAP, partner, and third-party applications. They provide a standardized approach that can be tailored to optimize systems and incorporate the latest cloud innovations.

To support this, we offer the SAP BTP Solution Diagram Repository on Draw.io, where you can create your own architectural landscapes. This tool allows you to visualize and extend your ideas, sparking inspiration and creativity, ideal for developers and architects working on future projects. You can find all of the available reference architectures in the new SAP Architecture Center.

When it comes to the know-how in SAP BTP, our Methodologies offer you a guided approach toward increasing your cloud technology maturity level, covering both technical and organizational aspects. As an architect, you might be interested in:

  • SAP Integration Solution Advisory Methodology: offers a framework that includes integration use case patterns, architecture blueprints, and additional best practices for cloud and hybrid IT landscapes. Its scope isn’t limited to technical aspects, but also covers the organizational dimension of enterprise integration like setting up an Integration Center of Excellence and defining integration roles including their tasks.
  • SAP Application Extension Methodology: provides a structured, technology-agnostic approach for customers and partners to define an organization-specific extension strategy. If you face complex challenges like ‘clean core’, ‘cloud readiness’, and ‘future proof’ concepts, you need to come up with a custom-tailored extension strategy for your organization. This methodology can help.
  • SAP Data and Analytics Advisory Methodology: provides a comprehensive framework for enterprise and data architects to evaluate data strategies, develop solution architectures, and foster a data-driven culture in a structured four-phase approach.

Navigating decisions on your own can be tough, especially when it comes to security. To ensure secure technology implementation, we provide Recommendations for safe configurations. Developers and administrators can learn how to set up and monitor these security recommendations using SAP Cloud ALM. These guidelines assist in evaluating the security of SAP BTP configurations.

Discover DevOps with SAP BTP and learn about how these principles enable your development and operations teams to be more efficient. Especially for developers and administrators, this will be a helpful tool to optimize workflows and bring out the best in your team. You can browse through the capabilities and determine what fits best for you by filtering for DevOps in the SAP Discovery Center Service Catalog.

As you navigate the complex landscape of technology adoption, it’s clear that the right guidance can make all the difference. With the right resources, you can transform the challenge of technology adoption into an opportunity for growth and innovation. Whether you’re building from the ground up or fine-tuning an existing system, the SAP BTP Guidance Framework will help you move forward with confidence.

You Might Also be Interested In

The Guidance Framework is one of many learning and enablement resources when it comes to SAP BTP. If you’re interested in exploring other expert-curated resources, check out my other blog post, SAP BTP Resource Rundown, where you can find more information about other top assets and enablement sources.

Earlier this year the SAP BTP Guidance Framework was presented as a live YouTube series with role-based episodes. Take a look at the recordings:

  1. SAP BTP Guidance Framework Series: How-to Empower Architects, Developers, and Administrators
  2. SAP BTP Guidance Framework: How-to Empower Developers
  3. SAP BTP Guidance Framework: How-to Empower Administrators

Stay Tuned

This blog post is just the beginning of a deep dive into the SAP BTP Guidance Framework. Coming next will be additional blog posts covering the different topic areas, written by the experts themselves. Stay tuned!

entry.title: Architecture Gone Wrong: Leveraging SAP Architecture Center for Better Outcomes
entry.content:
In the today growing software development, architecture plays a crucial role in determining the success and efficiency of a system. Poor architecture can lead to significant costs, performance issues, and maintenance headaches.

This blog explores the pitfalls of bad architecture and highlights the advantages of leveraging the SAP Architecture Center to ensure best practices and cost savings.

The Pitfalls of Poor Architecture

1. High Costs: Poorly designed architecture can lead to increased costs in several ways:

  • Maintenance: Systems with bad architecture require frequent fixes and updates, leading to higher maintenance costs.
  • Scalability: Inefficient architecture can struggle to scale, necessitating expensive overhauls or replacements.
  • Performance: Suboptimal architecture can result in slow performance, requiring additional resources to meet performance standards.

2. Performance Issues: Bad architecture can severely impact system performance:

  • Latency: Inefficient data flow and processing can lead to high latency, affecting user experience.
  • Reliability: Poor architecture can result in frequent downtimes and failures, reducing system reliability.
  • Resource Utilization: Inefficient use of resources can lead to higher operational costs and lower performance.

The SAP Architecture Center: A Solution

The SAP Architecture Center offers a comprehensive suite of tools, best practices, and reference architectures to help businesses design efficient and cost-effective systems

https://architecture.learning.sap.com 

Here are some key benefits:

1. Best Practices: The SAP Architecture Center provides access to industry best practices, ensuring that your architecture is designed to meet the highest standards. This includes guidelines for security, performance, and scalability.

2. Reference Architectures: Reference architectures are templates that outline the interactions between various services and components. They provide a standardized approach to designing systems, ensuring optimal configurations and seamless integration 

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/refArchCatalog/ 

3. Community-Driven Guidance: The SAP Architecture Center fosters a community of practice where architects and developers can share insights, collaborate, and learn from each other. This collaborative environment helps in adopting proven frameworks and innovative solutions

https://architecture.learning.sap.com/community/intro 

Advantages and Benefits

1. Cost Savings: By following SAP best practices and utilizing reference architectures, businesses can significantly reduce costs:

  • Reduced Maintenance: Well-designed architecture requires less frequent fixes and updates.
  • Efficient Resource Utilization: Optimal configurations ensure efficient use of resources, reducing operational costs.
  • Scalability: Scalable architecture minimizes the need for expensive overhauls.

2. Improved Performance: Leveraging the SAP Architecture Center can lead to better system performance:

  • Lower Latency: Efficient data flow and processing reduce latency.
  • Higher Reliability: Robust architecture ensures fewer downtimes and failures.
  • Optimal Resource Utilization: Efficient use of resources leads to better performance.

FinOps for Cost Saving

Financial Operations (FinOps) is a practice that combines financial management with cloud operations to optimize costs. Integrating FinOps with SAP best practices can further enhance cost savings:

  1. Cost Monitoring: FinOps tools help in monitoring cloud spending, identifying cost drivers, and optimizing resource usage.
  2. Budgeting and Forecasting: FinOps practices enable accurate budgeting and forecasting, ensuring that financial resources are allocated efficiently.
  3. Continuous Improvement: FinOps encourages continuous improvement by regularly reviewing and optimizing cloud spending, leading to sustained cost savings.

Conclusion

Poor architecture can lead to significant costs and performance issues. However, by leveraging the SAP Architecture Center and adopting FinOps practices, businesses can design efficient, cost-effective, and high-performing systems. The SAP Architecture Center provides best practices, reference architectures, and community-driven guidance to ensure that your architecture is built to the highest standards. Embrace these tools and practices to avoid the pitfalls of bad architecture and achieve optimal results.

entry.title: Code Connect 2025: UI5con, reCAP, HANA Tech Con
entry.content:
This blog post summarizes the most important information about Code Connect 2025. It will be updated with further information (e.g. Event App, links to recordings...).

Code Connect 2025 – three days, three conferences, one streamlined journey

Join us for the second annual  Code Connect, where we're bringing together the hottest tech events in one convenient location. Be ready to connect, collaborate, and code away with a bustling community of like-minded developers. Jump on the Code Connect train and elevate your conference experience!

  • Find more information here: https://code-connect.dev/ 
  • Event App: link

Code Jams & Warmup

UI5con

reCAP

HANA Tech Con

7th of July8th of July9th of July10th of July
code-connect.devopenui5.org/ui5conrecap-conf.devhanatech.community
See details in text belowAgendaAgendaAgenda
Live Streams: n/aLive Stream (Main Stage)

Live Stream (Main Stage)

Live Stream (Side Track)

Live Stream (Main Stage)

Live Stream (Side Track)

Recordings: n/aRecordings (will be added)Recordings (will be added) Recordings (will be added)

Code Jams & Warmup (July 7th)

Are you passionate about front-end development and have experience with OpenUI5 applications? Then don’t miss out on our OpenUI5-focused CodeJam! Join Peter Muessig and Nico Schönteich as they dive into advanced OpenUI5 topics. You’ll experience TypeScript, setting up a mono repository, OData V4 caveats, integrating 3rd-party packages, creating custom controls and libraries, and testing strategies.

Prefer back-end development? Our second CodeJam is tailored just for you! Led by DJ Adams, Daniel Schlachter, and Johannes Vogt, this session will explore the powerful SAP Application Programming Model (CAP). You’ll learn to integrate external services, work with CDS for declaring and relating entities, and leverage the SAP Cloud SDK for connectivity and destinations. In addition, we’ll also dive into new CAP features. Check the essential prerequisites before arriving at the event location.

Join the Official Unofficial Pre-Event for Code Connect 2025!

  • Right after the UI5- and CAP-CodeJams - starting from 05:00 PM on Monday, July 7
  • Location: Restaurant ACE at Golf Club at St. Leon Rot, just a short walk from ROT03
  • See more details here

  (July 8th)

Your journey to UI5 mastery begins at UI5con, the ultimate destination for developers of all levels.

Get ready to navigate through a network of knowledge, discover the latest UI5 innovations, and make new friendships along the way. With a map full of expert sessions and interactive workshops, you'll connect with the UI5 community and its creators, exchanging valuable insights and experiences. Are ready to dive into the UI5 universe? UI5con is your ticket to ride!

Learn more about UI5con

 (July 9th)

entry.title: Simplifying Complex Enterprise Landscapes Using SAP Integration Suite – A Practical Approach
entry.content:
“Complexity is inevitable. Chaos isn’t.”

In today’s enterprise IT landscape, complexity is everywhere — hybrid architectures, ever-evolving business needs, legacy platforms, and disconnected systems. But complexity doesn’t have to mean confusion or fragility.

As a Senior SAP Integration Architect with over 11 years of experience across SAP CPI, PI/PO, BTP, and Ariba CIG, I’ve seen firsthand how integration complexity can slow innovation — and how the right architectural mindset can fix it.

This post is a practical guide to simplifying complex enterprise landscapes using the SAP Integration Suite.

🔹 Why Complexity Happens

Most organizations face at least one (if not all) of these challenges:

  • A mix of cloud and on-premise systems

  • Legacy, point-to-point integrations built without standards

  • Business logic scattered across different platforms

  • Siloed teams and lack of visibility across integrations

The result? High maintenance, fragile interfaces, slower time-to-market.

🔹 My Guiding Principles for Simplification

1️⃣ API-First Architecture

Design integrations as modular, discoverable services — not tightly coupled one-offs. Use SAP API Management to expose, secure, and govern your interfaces centrally.

2️⃣ Decouple Business Logic from Integration

Avoid embedding decision logic in CPI flows or PI/PO mappings. Move that logic to CAP apps, BRF+, or reusable business services — this reduces rework and improves clarity.

3️⃣ Think Event-Driven

Don’t force synchronous flows where asynchronous is better. Use SAP Event Mesh to build decoupled, reactive systems that scale more naturally.

4️⃣ Reuse Over Rebuild

Standardize error handling, logging, naming conventions, and message formats. Use templates, shared artifacts, and integration accelerators wherever possible.

5️⃣ Integration as a Platform

Treat integration as a long-term platform, not a project. Build with future growth, maintainability, and observability in mind.

🔹 Using SAP Integration Suite Holistically

Most teams focus only on SAP CPI — but the SAP Integration Suite is more powerful when used as a full toolkit:

  • Cloud Integration (CPI): For process orchestration across SAP and non-SAP systems

  • API Management: To secure, manage, and reuse interfaces

  • SAP Managed Gateway (Ariba CIG): For rapid, standardized B2B procurement integration

  • Event Mesh: To enable scalable, event-driven communication

  • SAP BTP Cockpit & Cloud ALM: For centralized monitoring, alerting, and operational control

Together, these tools can reduce integration effort, increase visibility, and support long-term agility.

🔹 Real-World Tactics That Worked for Me

Here are some of the steps I’ve implemented in customer landscapes:

  • Migrated legacy PI/PO integrations to CPI on SAP BTP

  • Created custom adapters and reusable Groovy-based modules

  • Designed a central error-handling and alerting framework

  • Introduced integration naming standards, templates, and design guidelines

  • Used Cloud ALM to monitor interfaces across landscapes

  • Built a knowledge base of reusable integration components

🔹 Final Thoughts

Complexity is natural. But without intentional design, it turns into chaos.

By adopting a product mindset, focusing on API-first, reusable, and event-driven architecture, and fully utilizing the SAP Integration Suite, we can turn fragile systems into future-ready platforms.

Let’s not just connect systems — let’s design integration that scales with the product, not against it.

🔁 Let’s Discuss

How are you approaching simplification in your integration landscape?
Would love to hear your views and lessons from the field.

entry.title: Announcement: SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud Availability via SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) is a fully managed SaaS solution that unifies and governs all SAP data and seamlessly connects with third-party data, giving line-of-business leaders context to make even more impactful decisions.

Learn more: SAP Business Data Cloud | SAP

SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud are core component services of BDC.  Both services remain strategic to SAP and will continue to be enhanced through their own roadmaps, offered via SAP Business Data Cloud.

SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud are scheduled to be removed from the list of Eligible Cloud Services for new BTPEA, CPEA, and PAYG subscriptions as of January 1, 2026.  

What does this mean for customers? 

These services will be available until the end of a customer’s current BTPEA, CPEA, or PAYG agreement terms.  After December 31, 2025, these services will not be available for renewal. While the services will not be available for renewal terms, the same services will continue to be available in SAP Business Data Cloud.

Existing BTPEA and CPEA customers who purchase cloud credits before January 1, 2026, will be able to continue to use their cloud credits for SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud for the duration of their agreements.

Although customers can continue their current BTPEA, CPEA, and PAYG agreements, there is no need to wait to move to SAP Business Data Cloud.  Customers can now begin their transition plans to SAP BDC.  Please contact your SAP Account team to support your plans.

Note: Existing SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud tenants will be preserved under SAP BDC, no technical migration required. 

 

SAP Analytics Cloud in this blog refers to the following:

  • SAP Analytics Cloud for business intelligence, public system option
  • SAP Analytics Cloud for planning, standard edition, public system option
  • SAP Analytics Cloud for planning, professional edition, public system option
  • SAP Analytics Cloud, test tenant, public system option

entry.title: Say Hi to Joule in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile, Powered by SAP Build - Part 1: Unlocking AI for HR
entry.content:
This two-part blog series explores how SAP SuccessFactors Mobile introduced SAP Joule - SAP's AI copilot - into the hands of mobile users. Part one introduces the opportunity, motivation, and foundational changes that made this integration possible. Part two explores the architectural evolution and how SAP Mobile Services enabled the delivery of this new experience.

SAP SuccessFactors is a market-leading Human Capital Management (HCM) solution, trusted by over 9,900 organizations and more than 260 million users globally. Designed to help organizations attract, develop, and retain their workforce, SAP SuccessFactors offers a comprehensive suite of tools to manage the entire employee lifecycle. 

Complementing the web experience, SAP SuccessFactors Mobile brings HR closer to employees - driving higher engagement, productivity, and informed decision-making. It delivers a native, consumer-grade experience while meeting strict enterprise-grade security standards. With performance and usability at its core, the app is tailored to meet the demands of modern mobile users. 

SAP Joule is an AI copilot grounded in customers' business data and infused with AI agents to proactively assist employees in every cloud application they use - while automating complex processes. 

With the 1H2025 release, SuccessFactors has introduced the Joule experience in its mobile app. This means employees can now interact with the entire enterprise suite using natural language directly from the SuccessFactors mobile app - whether they want to find information, complete a task, or navigate to the right location. 

Joule supports three primary capabilities for HR scenarios: transactional, navigational, and informational.  

  • In transactional scenarios, Joule not only retrieves data - it facilitates actions. For example, when a user says, "I want to request feedback from my manager," Joule collects the necessary context and sends the request directly within the same conversational thread. 
  • In navigational cases, Joule acts as a smart shortcut, directing users to the appropriate screen inside the app. For instance, if you’re unsure where to assign team goals, Joule will generate a direct deep link to the right page. 
  • For informational scenarios, Joule queries its underlying documentation and knowledge base, then presents a concise summary along with related resources. Ask it about your company’s leave policy, and it will display a brief overview plus the top three relevant documents. 

Joule in the SuccessFactors Mobile app isn't limited to HR scenarios. Users can also query data from connected systems like SAP S/4HANA or Ariba - for example, to view open sales orders or pending purchase requests. 

Joule can even be launched via Siri on iOS. Users can say, “Hey Siri, ask Joule in SuccessFactors…,” which opens the app and passes the query to Joule. Joule then processes and responds within the app - creating a seamless, hands-free experience. 

With SuccessFactors' capabilities rapidly expanding on the web, customers will now begin to see the same momentum in the mobile app. Joule's integration empowers users on the go to access not only HR information but also broader SAP business content - bringing the power of one unified copilot across SAP systems into the SuccessFactors mobile experience. 

 

                        

                                              

One of the core goals was to unlock a best-in-class user experience. From the beginning, it was clear that users who had already onboarded to the SuccessFactors mobile app shouldn't need to re-authenticate when accessing Joule. Achieving this required alignment across authentication models. While the mobile app previously used IDP activation protocols, Joule required IAS-based tokens. 

These authentication changes were part of a much larger transformation across the SuccessFactors mobile architecture. In part two, we’ll explore how SAP Mobile Services and its SDKs enabled this journey - and what it means for the future. 

Co-authored by @VinodDevarajan @sandeeptds  @Xeric @SamZhang @SamiLechner @_dk @ptawade

entry.title: Say Hi to Joule in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile, Powered by SAP Build — Part 2: Architecture, Execution
entry.content:
In the first part of this blog series, we explored how SAP Joule was introduced into the SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app and what it means for users. In this follow-up, we dive deeper into the architecture, execution approach, and the outlook for the mobile experience. 

SAP BTP is a platform engineered to integrate, automate, extend, and build AI-powered business applications and processes across the enterprise. SAP Build is the unified application development and process automation solution on SAP BTP, enabling personalization and efficiency at scale.

SAP Mobile Services is the channel for mobile development in SAP Build. Developers can use the Mobile Development Kit or SAP BTP SDK for iOS or SAP BTP SDK for Android to create robust, native apps. Administrators can manage the full app lifecycle - from configuring push notifications and securing data to enabling offline access and monitoring usage. SAP apps such as SAP Mobile Start, SAP Ariba, and SAP Service and Asset Manager are all built on this foundational technology stack.

 

The SAP BTP SDK for iOS and SAP BTP SDK for Android provide a reusable component enabling SAP apps to deliver a consistent and native Joule experience across mobile platforms. SAP Mobile Start also integrated this component to bring Joule to its users last year.

SAP SuccessFactors Mobile has long leveraged the Fiori frameworks from the BTP SDKs to provide a consumer-grade experience in an enterprise context. These frameworks follow Fiori design guidelines while maintaining a fully native feel on iOS and Android. Given this deep integration with the BTP SDKs, adopting the Joule component was a natural progression.

As outlined in the first blog, SAP SuccessFactors Mobile supports multiple onboarding mechanisms - QR code, deep link, company search, secret code, and MDM-based provisioning. While this flexibility has been key to serving a diverse user base, it also meant that delivering a seamless, SSO-based Joule experience required a significant architectural shift.

To meet this challenge, SuccessFactors leveraged SAP Mobile Services and the SAP BTP SDKs for iOS and Android. This allowed for secure authentication and efficient routing of Joule-related traffic while maintaining compatibility with existing onboarding flows. The change was foundational - requiring careful planning to avoid disruption for millions of active users worldwide. SAP SuccessFactors Mobile app now connects to both the SuccessFactors (for standard HR data/functions via APIs) and to the Joule service for AI queries.

A key decision in the SAP Mobile Services adoption journey was determining how SAP SuccessFactors Mobile Server would communicate with SAP Mobile Services. A lightweight multi-tenancy model was adopted, allowing SAP Mobile Services to operate behind the scenes while preserving the familiar admin experience within SuccessFactors. New APIs were introduced to maintain this continuity while supporting increased scale.

The adoption of SAP Mobile Services had significant implications for SuccessFactors' client logic and the SAP BTP SDKs for iOS and Android. For instance, the SuccessFactors app transitioned to using the SecureStore Module provided by the BTP SDKs, which securely manages and stores sensitive PII user information on mobile devices. By leveraging this module, SuccessFactors eliminated the need to maintain its own database and reduced reliance on third-party libraries. Another example is that SuccessFactors required administrators to configure preferred browsers via MDM and enforce digits-only passcodes. To accommodate these requirements, the SAP BTP SDKs introduced new capabilities in its Foundation and FioriFlows frameworks.

This collaboration offered mutual benefits, as features developed in response to SuccessFactors' needs were implemented in a reusable way, including enhanced MDM support and new authentication capabilities. As a result, these features became part of the Mobile Services platform offering, benefiting not only other SAP apps but also external customers and partners building their own mobile solutions with SAP Build. The effort also enabled SuccessFactors to audit and simplify its own mobile app, streamlining processes in some cases by retiring underused functionality or tightening security – for instance, limiting the ability to delete other profiles to the primary user.

A massive undertaking of this scope required a phased approach from SuccessFactors, even for features already supported. Future releases will enable SuccessFactors to enable Joule on shared devices.

The foundation laid so far has set the stage for long-term collaboration. For example, SuccessFactors' mobile apps will soon feature an updated home page built using BTP Fiori SDK UI components. We're also planning to enable users to capture photos of documents in response to HR flows in Joule for use cases where attaching certificates or receipts is required. Once synergies are fully realized, SuccessFactors will be well-positioned to leverage more SAP Mobile Services capabilities such as offline functionality. Another exciting area of joint exploration is on-device AI models. As mobile devices become increasingly powerful, both teams are keen to investigate how AI workloads can be intelligently distributed between the cloud and the device - unlocking even more responsive and personalized user experiences.

Please note that all future items mentioned here are indicative and may change subject to SAP's strategic interests.

Beyond the features themselves, this collaborative effort continues to drive SAP’s broader strategic vision of being AI-first and Suite-first. With Joule now live in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile, and with a strong outlook ahead, the foundation is set for a smarter, more unified SAP experience - on every device, for every user.

 

Note: This article has been co-authored by @VinodDevarajan @ptawade @Xeric @SamZhang @SamiLechner @_dk

entry.title: Beyond SAP Cloud - Episode 29: Cloud Identity Services Introduction
entry.content:
In this episode, we explore Cloud Identity Service, a crucial tool for securing BTP environments. It includes three key services: Identity Authentication, Identity Directory, and Identity Provisioning. These services enable single sign-on, manage user authorization, and streamline user provisioning from various systems. The authentication service connects your corporate identity provider to ensure seamless access across cloud solutions, while the directory manages user groups and roles. Provisioning automates user creation and synchronization. By using Cloud Identity Services, organizations can enhance security, improve user management, and simplify integration across multiple platforms.

Takeaways

  • Identity Cloud Service is made up of three key services: Identity Authentication, Identity Provisioning, and Identity Directory.
  • Identity Authentication service helps secure the environment by integrating with your corporate identity provider for single sign-on (SSO) capabilities.
  • Cloud Identity service acts as a proxy between BTP (Business Technology Platform) and the corporate identity provider, enabling seamless authentication.
  • Without a corporate identity provider, you won't be able to set up SSO, but it is possible to authenticate without it.
  • The goal of Cloud Identity services is to allow integration across all SAP cloud solutions, ensuring consistent authentication through a single token.
  • Cloud Identity service offers additional security features such as two-factor authentication, risk-based authentication, and conditional authentication.
  • Conditional authentication can support different authentication strategies for internal vs external users, such as routing internal users to a corporate identity provider and external users to Cloud Identity.
  • The Identity Directory service stores user information within Cloud Identity Services, but this is not mandatory for all use cases. It can be enabled if needed.
  • Newer services like Built Apps and SuccessFactors require user data to be available in Cloud Identity, making it important to enable the user store when using those services.
  • Identity Provisioning allows users to be provisioned from external systems (e.g., S/4 HANA) into Cloud Identity's identity directory using the SCIM protocol.
  • Even though single sign-on can be set up without Cloud Identity services by directly integrating with a corporate identity provider, using Cloud Identity services is recommended for better efficiency and support for new services in BTP.
